summary: Matched src/melee/vi/vi.c, replacing the 350-line asm/melee/vi/vi.s stub with C for four vi functions and the 26-entry character file-name table. The PR also switched vi.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py and from asm object to C object in obj_files.mk, added vi.h prototypes, and tightened several dependent prototypes in camera, game-mode, audio, and lb headers. The author credited Cortex's matching work plus their own typing fixes and character table setup. There was no recorded review discussion.

key_files.why: Deleted the previous hand-written assembly file containing un_8031C99C, un_8031C9B4, un_8031CA04, un_8031CAAC, and the associated data strings/table.
Added the matched C implementation, including the 26-element un_803FFFA8 character data-file table and four vi functions.
Added platform and HSD_GObj includes plus prototypes for the newly decompiled vi functions.
Replaced asm/melee/vi/vi.s.o with src/melee/vi/vi.c.o in TEXT_O_FILES.
Typed Camera_800313E0 as void Camera_800313E0(HSD_GObj*, u64), enabling the matched call site.
Typed gm_801678F8 as void gm_801678F8(s32, s32, s32) and gm_8017E440 as u8 gm_8017E440(void).
Corrected lbAudioAx_800236DC from taking HSD_GObj* to taking no arguments.
Updated an existing caller to lbAudioAx_800236DC() after the prototype correction.
Large symbol update, mostly local symbol renumbering and vi data/function symbol effects caused by compiling vi.c as matching C.

what_changed: Reconstructed un_803FFFA8 as a global char* table with 26 entries such as "PlCaDViWaitAJ.dat", "PlDkDViWaitAJ.dat", and "PlGnDViWaitAJ.dat".
Implemented un_8031C99C(s32 index) as a simple table lookup returning un_803FFFA8[index].
Implemented un_8031C9B4(s32 arg0, s32 arg1) by calling gm_8017E440(), checking for mode value 4, and otherwise forwarding the mode plus two arguments to gm_801678F8.

Implemented un_8031CA04(HSD_GObj* arg0) by cycling Camera_800310A0 through 2, 1, and 0, setting arg0->gxlink_prios to 9 then 8, calling HSD_GObj_80390ED0(arg0, 7), and finishing with Camera_800313E0(arg0, 1).
Implemented un_8031CAAC(void) by calling gm_801A36A0(gm_8017E430()), checking bit 0x1000, then invoking lbAudioAx_800236DC, lbAudioAx_80023694, lb_800145F4, and gm_801A4B60 when set.
Converted vi.c to a Matching object and routed the build from the assembly object to the C object.
Adjusted function prototypes in several headers to remove UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and reflect no-argument or typed-argument call signatures needed by vi.c.
Updated mnsoundtest.c to match the corrected no-argument lbAudioAx_800236DC signature.
smart_moves: Matched both code and data: the PR did not only translate functions, it rebuilt the character data-file pointer table that had previously existed as separate .data string objects plus a pointer table in assembly.

Used real function signatures instead of forcing casts or leaving UNK_PARAMS, improving type information for future callers of gm_801678F8, gm_8017E440, Camera_800313E0, lb_800145F4, and lbAudioAx_800236DC.
Propagated the lbAudioAx_800236DC prototype fix to an existing caller in mnsoundtest.c, avoiding a stale argument at another call site.
Made the build-system transition complete by updating both configure.py and obj_files.mk while deleting the old asm file.
Preserved exact string spelling in the character table, including the unusual lowercase-j entry "PlMsDViWaitAj.dat", which matters for binary/data matching.
decomp_lessons: When matching a small file, include the associated data objects in the same C translation unit if the asm file contained both .text and .data; pointer tables and string literals can be essential for the object to match.
Prototype cleanup is often part of a match. Replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with precise return and parameter types can remove call-site ambiguity and produce the expected codegen.
A no-argument function prototype can affect existing callers outside the target file; search for callers and update them in the same PR.
For HSD_GObj-related code, field names such as gxlink_prios can express raw offset writes from assembly more cleanly than manual struct-offset work.

Large symbols.txt churn may come from local compiler-generated symbol renumbering after a file is compiled as C; treat these updates as supporting evidence rather than separate feature work unless the diff shows semantic symbol additions.
Keep magic values visible when the original semantics are still unknown: this match retained values like mode 4, camera IDs 2/1/0, gxlink priority 9/8, link value 7, and bit mask 0x1000.
naming_conventions: The file retained address-based unknown names for functions and globals: un_8031C99C, un_8031C9B4, un_8031CA04, un_8031CAAC, and un_803FFFA8.
New prototypes in vi.h use address comments in the project style, e.g. /* 31C99C */ char* un_8031C99C(s32);
External symbols kept their subsystem prefixes and address suffixes, such as Camera_800313E0, gm_801678F8, gm_8017E440, lbAudioAx_800236DC, and lb_800145F4.
The character table entries follow the PlXXDViWaitAJ.dat asset-name pattern, with the evidence showing one exact-case exception: PlMsDViWaitAj.dat.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Assembly table lookup `slwi index, 2` plus load from un_803FFFA8 was represented directly as `return un_803FFFA8[index];`.
The original assembly's 26 separate string objects plus pointer table were collapsed into a C `char* un_803FFFA8[26]` initializer, preserving data order.
The `gm_8017E440` result was stored in a u8 local before comparison to 4, matching the assembly's clrlwi byte behavior.
The 64-bit result path in un_8031CAAC was represented with a `u64 temp_ret` and `(temp_ret & 0x1000) != 0`, matching the assembly's high/low-word bit-test intent.
Repeated camera and GObj calls in un_8031CA04 were kept explicit rather than abstracted into a loop, which is often necessary for exact compiler output in small matching functions.
Build matching required deleting the asm object and switching both build declarations to the C object.

review_feedback.note: The PR context reports zero issue comments, review comments, and reviews; no human reviewer feedback was available.
The body states: "Cortex's work to get this matched with some fixes from me for typing, plus getting the character table setup."

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document un_8031C99C, un_8031C9B4, un_8031CA04, un_8031CAAC, or un_803FFFA8.
Search for follow-up corrections to Camera_800313E0(HSD_GObj*, u64) or gm_801A36A0 return typing.
Check whether the extra standalone semicolon after lb_800145F4 in lb_00F9.h was later cleaned up.
Look for PRs that replace the PlXXDViWaitAJ.dat table with a named character asset table or enum-indexed table.
